title: metastelma rupicola
intro: Metastelma rupicola, also known as the rockvine, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Metastelma rupicola include its potential to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as a wound healing agent. Herbal preparations of Metastelma rupicola typically involve the plant's leaves and stems being dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a topical ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
